steps.title: Find the axis (pencil)
steps.description: Lightly sketch a single vertical line where the center of the bucket will stand. The bucket will balance around this axis.

steps.title: Block the body (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, sketch two slightly angled lines on either side of the axis for the bucket's sides — wider at the top than the bottom. Add a flattened oval across the top opening, and a shallow curve across the bottom.

steps.title: Draw through the back of the rim (pencil)
steps.description: Lightly sketch the back half of the rim oval — the part that sits behind the front lip. Drawing the full ellipse keeps the opening feeling round, not pinched.

steps.title: Plan the handle (pencil)
steps.description: Sketch a curved arc rising from one side of the rim, looping over, and coming down to the other side. Mark two small dots where the handle meets the rim for the attachment points.

steps.title: Block the fish (pencil)
steps.description: Inside the rim, sketch two or three overlapping curved shapes for fish poking out of the catch — a tail here, a head there. They tumble against each other; no two should look identical.

steps.title: Switch to ink and trace the bucket
steps.description: Switch to ink. Trace the body of the bucket and the front half of the rim in confident strokes. Use a slightly heavier line along the bottom and the lower side of the body — that weight grounds the bucket.

steps.title: Ink the handle and rim attachment (ink)
steps.description: Still in ink, trace the handle in one curving pass. At each attachment dot, add a small loop or rivet mark where the handle meets the rim. Land the lines cleanly at the junctions.

steps.title: Ink the fish (ink)
steps.description: Trace each fish silhouette with confident strokes. Where one fish sits in front of another, stop the back fish's outline cleanly where the front one crosses it. Add a small dot for each visible eye and a few short marks for gill lines.

steps.title: Anchor with a cast shadow (ink)
steps.description: Beneath the bucket, draw a flattened ellipse for the cast shadow — wider than the base and offset slightly to one side, following the angle of the sun. A few short parallel strokes inside the ellipse give it weight.
